title: OpenAI considers burying the chat app as intelligence finds its level - TCR 06/08/26
description:
OpenAI is recasting ChatGPT into an agent-and-coding superapp ahead of its IPO, rival labs are invading each other's markets while enterprises route routine work to cheaper models, and MIT engineers embedded gallium nitride transistors into lab-grown diamond to build a wireless amplifier that beat every comparable device in the literature.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-8-2026/

0:00 OpenAI recasts ChatGPT as the labs invade each other
2:02 The AI answer layer becomes a fraud surface
16:17 Claude reads NMR spectra at the chemistry bench
20:51 Close

This episode covers the OpenAI ChatGPT superapp pivot and model-routing cost collapse, three competing blueprints for who owns AI's upside, the AI answer layer becoming a fraud delivery surface, Aviva's record £233m in suspect claims, MIT's gallium nitride on diamond breakthrough, and Anthropic showing Claude reading NMR spectra back to the molecule.

The spine of the edition is one shape: intelligence finding its level as the value of any single position erodes. OpenAI is abandoning the conversational interface that made it famous to chase Anthropic's enterprise lane, and every lab is racing into every other's territory because capability is commoditizing faster than any moat can hold. Corporate buyers route hard problems to frontier models and routine work to cheaper ones that answer just as well, draining the premium across most of the volume. That leveling is why three rival ownership blueprints - a government stake, a 50 percent equity transfer, and a publicly run AI option - arrived in the same cycle. On the science side, MIT cooled the heat ceiling on next-generation wireless by routing computation onto diamond, Anthropic moved an emerging intelligence toward the chemistry bench, and a genome-stratified trial turned an approved drug into targeted prevention. Cloned storefronts surfaced inside ChatGPT shopping answers while insurers turned the same generative analysis back on AI-staged fraud.
